model_input:
You are a DVOC (Draft-Verify-Action Loop) verifier. A red crosshair shows the current predicted click position on the screen. A fixed blue circle provides spatial reference.

Call report_offset(dx, dy, confidence) with the pixel offset from the crosshair to the target element center.
dx: pixels right (negative = left). dy: pixels down (negative = up).
confidence: 0.0 (uncertain) to 1.0 (certain).

Only report dx=0, dy=0 when the crosshair is within a few pixels of the target. If uncertain, set lower confidence so the loop keeps refining.

Task: Click the Beta project card
Previous iterations:
  iter 0: at (184,426) said dx=242 dy=-318 conf=1.00 -> (426,108)
  iter 1: at (426,108) said dx=-40 dy=170 conf=1.00 -> (386,278)
  iter 2: at (406,193) said dx=66 dy=8 conf=1.00 -> (472,201)
  iter 3: at (422,195) said dx=-35 dy=85 conf=1.00 -> (388,280)
  iter 4: at (414,216) said dx=60 dy=-20 conf=1.00 -> (474,196)
Call report_offset().

raw_message.reasoning_content:
The user wants me to click the "Beta" project card.
The current crosshair (red circle with cross) is at (428, 211) as per the bottom right label (approximately).
Looking at the image, the "Beta" project card is the second one in the first row.
The center of the Beta project card:
- The card spans roughly from x=270 to x=500 and y=180 to y=370.
- The text "Beta" is around x=300, y=300.
- The icon is around x=310, y=240.
- The center of the card would be roughly x=(270+500)/2 = 385, y=(180+370)/2 = 275.
Wait, looking at the image, the crosshair is already on the "Beta" card.
Let's re-estimate the card's center.
The card's boundaries:
Left: ~268
Right: ~502
Top: ~184
Bottom: ~372
Center X: (268 + 502) / 2 = 385
Center Y: (184 + 372) / 2 = 278

Current crosshair position: (428, 211).
Offset dx = 385 - 428 = -43
Offset dy = 278 - 211 = 67

Let me refine the target center.
The card "Beta" contains:
- A gear icon at (310, 240)
- Text "Beta" at (300, 300)
- Description at (350, 330)
The center of the whole white card area seems to be around (385, 278).
Actually, just clicking anywhere within the card should work, but the goal is the center.
Crosshair is at (428, 211).
Target is at (385, 278).
dx = 385 - 428 = -43
dy = 278 - 211 = 67

Wait, let's check the crosshair again. It's quite close to the top center of the Beta card.
Actually, the crosshair is at (428, 211).
The Beta card is the one with the green border.
Left edge of Beta card: ~268
Right edge: ~502
Top edge: ~184
Bottom edge: ~372
Center: (385, 278)
dx = 385 - 428 = -43
dy = 278 - 211 = 67

Let me double check coordinates.
The total width is 1000.
Beta card starts at about 27% (270) and ends at about 50% (500).
Top is at about 18% (180) and bottom is at about 37% (370).
Center is roughly (385, 275).
Crosshair is at (428, 211).
dx = 385 - 428 = -43
dy = 275 - 211 = 64.

Let's re-evaluate.
The Beta card is bounded by x=[268, 502], y=[184, 372].
Center x = 385, y = 278.
Crosshair (428, 211).
dx = 385 - 428 = -43.
dy = 278 - 211 = 67.
Confidence: 1.0.
